# signal-first-research

> A Claude Code skill (MIT) that guides the design of research intake filters admitting only information likely to change your next action. Refuses both volume bias (a guilt queue that outruns reading time) and false-abstinence (reading nothing because sorting feels too expensive). Core rule: define the signal before the search.

## Core documentation

- [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt): self-contained Q&A reference for AI agents (Project Facts, 8 question-form H2 sections)
- [README.md](README.md): human-facing narrative (install, how it works, failure-mode table)
- [SKILL.md](skills/signal-first-research/SKILL.md): full skill specification — define-signal-first rule, search-wide/intake-narrow asymmetry, three worked filter shapes, diagnostic checklist

## Architecture

- Core rule: **define the signal before the search** — name the next action the research informs, write the signal in terms of that action, admit only items matching it
- Asymmetry: search phase is generous and broad (machines can do it); intake phase is strict and binary (only the reader can decide) — no "maybe" piles, no "save for later"
- Three worked filter shapes: **daily topic digest** (top-N hard cap), **session-scoped filter** (no deferred reads), **exploration mode** (named, time-boxed exception that breaks the rule on purpose)
- Diagnostic checklist: six questions; more than two "no" answers means the filter is broken
- Design lineage: the "how" counterpart to [AKC ADR-0010 Human Cognitive Resource as Central Constraint](https://github.com/shimo4228/agent-knowledge-cycle/blob/main/docs/adr/0010-human-cognitive-resource-as-central-constraint.md)
- Sync model: canonical copy lives in the author's Claude Code harness; this repo is a one-way mirror via `scripts/sync-from-local.sh`

## Standard reference

- [Agent Skill specification](https://agentskills.io/specification): the Claude Code skill format used by this repo
- [SkillsMP marketplace](https://agentskills.io/specification): one-step install via `/skills add`

## Sibling skills

- [search-first](https://github.com/shimo4228/search-first): research-before-coding workflow — distinct role: search-first guards the moment before writing code, this skill designs the intake of recurring research workflows
- [daily-research](https://github.com/shimo4228/daily-research): cron-driven daily research digest — the production deployment of this pattern
- [agent-knowledge-cycle](https://github.com/shimo4228/agent-knowledge-cycle): the AKC research line this skill belongs to ([DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19200726](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19200726))
